lede: A look at August 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Héctor Fort and Kerry Rhodes, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2014 Kunshan explosion
events.desc: At least 146 people were killed and more than 114 injured in a factory explosion in Kunshan, Jiangsu, China.

events.title: Helsinki Metro
events.desc: The Helsinki Metro, the first rapid transit system of Finland, is opened to the general public.

events.title: Summerland disaster
events.desc: A flash fire kills 50 people at the Summerland amusement centre at Douglas, Isle of Man.

events.title: Albert Einstein
events.desc: Albert Einstein and Leo Szilard write a letter to Franklin D. Roosevelt, urging him to begin the Manhattan Project to develop a nuclear weapon.

events.title: Calvin Coolidge
events.desc: U.S. Vice President Calvin Coolidge becomes president upon the death of President Warren G. Harding.

events.title: Austria-Hungary
events.desc: World War I: Austrian sabotage causes the sinking of the Italian battleship Leonardo da Vinci in Taranto.

events.title: Clay Street Hill Railroad
events.desc: The Clay Street Hill Railroad begins operating the first cable car in San Francisco's famous cable car system.

events.title: Tower Subway
events.desc: Tower Subway, the world's first underground tube railway, opens in London, England, United Kingdom.

events.title: 1790 United States census
events.desc: The first United States Census is conducted.

events.title: Thomas Grey (conspirator)
events.desc: Thomas Grey is executed for participating in the Southampton Plot.

births.name: Rose Tremain
births.line: English novelist and short story writer

births.name: Ravishankar Shukla
births.line: Indian lawyer and politician, 1st Chief Minister of Madhya Pradesh (died 1956)

deaths.text: Nitin Chandrakant Desai, Indian art director, production designer, and film and television producer
Eduard Magnus Jakobson, Estonian missionary and engraver

observances.text: Christian feast day: Basil Fool for Christ (Russian Orthodox Church)
